step_rationale: Eicosanoid enzyme; inflammation resolution altered post-MI; leukotriene pathway.
mechanism: 5-Lipoxygenase produces leukotrienes that regulate inflammatory cell recruitment and resolution post-MI.

references.title: Arachidonic acid fuels inflammation by unlocking macrophage protein phosphatase 5 after myocardial infarction.
Metabolomics study of the effect of smoking and high-fat diet on metabolic responses and related mechanism following myocardial infarction in mice.
Obesity superimposed on aging magnifies inflammation and delays the resolving response after myocardial infarction.
references.journal: American journal of physiology. Heart and circulatory physiology

trials.title: Role of the Arachidonate 5-Lipoxygenase Pathway in Coronary Heart Disease
A Phase 2 Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study of the Effect of VIA-2291, a 5-Lipoxygenase Inhibitor, on Vascular Inflammation in Patients After an Acute Coronary Syndrome Event
